source_jobs.title: I don't fit into any of these roles!
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT COLUMN

For companies building financial technology and transforming the financial services space, the biggest bottleneck to their growth and innovation is often the underlying banks and infrastructure stack they rely on. We have spent our careers founding and scaling companies like Plaid, Square, Meta, Blend, and Affirm, and have seen this problem firsthand — builders and developers needing to partner with traditional banks, and creating API and abstraction layers over the patchwork that is the bank, its core, and many other vendors. All of this results in a complex (and often expensive) banking supply chain involving a user, fintech, BaaS middleware provider, bank, core and the Federal Reserve.


At Column, we set out to simplify and fix this. We are a bank and a software company built from the ground up, offering builders and developers technology-forward banking solutions that cut out the hundreds of vendors, middleware providers, and abstraction layers. This means a safer, more transparent, and less costly banking supply chain. Come build with us!



We're mostly hiring locally in the San Francisco Bay Area, however will be open to remote for exceptional candidates. Sadly we won't have the time to respond to everyone, but we do read every application we get. Please write about why you're interested and what role you're interested in. No need for a formal resume, just shoot your LinkedIn, Github or something similar.




WE LOOK FORWARD TO HEARING FROM YOU

Column is committed to working with the best and brightest people from the broadest talent pool possible. We value bringing together a team with different perspectives, educational backgrounds, and life experiences, and believe a diversity of ideas is what allows us to develop the best solutions. All qualified individuals are encouraged to apply.


If you need assistance or a reasonable accommodation during the application and recruiting process, please reach out to accommodations@column.com.



We participate in the E-Verify program in certain locations as required by law. Learn more about the E-Verify program here https://e-verify.gov.

source_jobs.title: Software Engineer
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT COLUMN

For companies building financial technology and transforming the financial services space, the biggest bottleneck to their growth and innovation is often the underlying banks and infrastructure stack they rely on. We have spent our careers founding and scaling companies like Plaid, Square, Meta, Blend, and Affirm, and have seen this problem firsthand — builders and developers needing to partner with traditional banks, and creating API and abstraction layers over the patchwork that is the bank, its core, and many other vendors. All of this results in a complex (and often expensive) banking supply chain involving a user, fintech, BaaS middleware provider, bank, core and the Federal Reserve.


At Column, we set out to simplify and fix this. We are a bank and a software company built from the ground up, offering builders and developers technology-forward banking solutions that cut out the hundreds of vendors, middleware providers, and abstraction layers. This means a safer, more transparent, and less costly banking supply chain. Come build with us!




THE OPPORTUNITY:

You’ll be responsible for building and maintaining the systems that power everything at Column - from our payments stack to developer tools to core platform services. This role is perfect for someone who thrives at the intersection of infrastructure, software, and financial systems. Expect to own core parts of our infrastructure and shape the technical foundation of a company moving billions of dollars per day.



Read more about Life @ Column and our hiring process here https://columncom.notion.site/Hiring-at-column-b8d9b2628317491989d4713091b6bb68. 🚀




WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Build and maintain critical infrastructure: compute, storage, networking, security, and observability systems

 - Own core banking infrastructure, including direct integrations with the Federal Reserve and real-time payment networks

 - Design and implement CI/CD pipelines, developer tooling, and release engineering workflows

 - Improve system resiliency and availability through better monitoring, testing, and automation

 - Lead platform initiatives like authentication/authorization, change data capture, and cross-cutting APIs

 - Operate close to the metal: optimize performance, reliability, and correctness across our systems

 - Uphold the highest standards for system safety, especially in a regulated, mission-critical environment
   
   


WHAT YOU’LL GET FROM US:

 - 🏥 Comprehensive health, dental, and vision plans, including options that are 100% covered by Column for you and 100% covered for your dependents!

 - 🐣 Comprehensive family planning and fertility benefits via partnership with Carrot, including reimbursement of up to $20,000 in qualified expenses

 - 🏡 Up to $2,000 (post-tax) monthly rent subsidy for employees living within 2 miles of Column’s office

 - 💳 FSA and HSA account options to enable use of pre-tax money for medical and dependent care expenses

 - 📈 401k plan, including self-directed brokerage options

 - 🌴 Flexible time-off policy - take the time off that you want and need to relax and recharge

 - 👶 100% paid parental leave, including 16 weeks for birth mothers, 12 weeks for primary caregivers, and 8 weeks for secondary caregivers

 - 😇 Up to $4,500 in annual reimbursements for backup childcare

 - 🍽️ Catered lunches and dinners for SF employees

 - 🚆 Commuter benefits, including paid transportation to-and-from the office

 - 🎉 Regular team building events, including annual offsite
   
   


PAY TRANSPARENCY:

Compensation packages at Column include base salary, equity, and benefits. New hire offers are made based on a candidate's experience, expertise, geographic location, and internal pay equity relative to peers.

The annual US base salary range for this role is $130,000-$250,000 + equity.




WE LOOK FORWARD TO HEARING FROM YOU

Column is committed to working with the best and brightest people from the broadest talent pool possible. We value bring

source_jobs.title: Growth
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT COLUMN

For companies building financial technology and transforming the financial services space, the biggest bottleneck to their growth and innovation is often the underlying banks and infrastructure stack they rely on. We have spent our careers founding and scaling companies like Plaid, Square, Meta, Blend, and Affirm, and have seen this problem firsthand — builders and developers needing to partner with traditional banks, and creating API and abstraction layers over the patchwork that is the bank, its core, and many other vendors. All of this results in a complex (and often expensive) banking supply chain involving a user, fintech, BaaS middleware provider, bank, core and the Federal Reserve.


At Column, we set out to simplify and fix this. We are a bank and a software company built from the ground up, offering builders and developers technology-forward banking solutions that cut out the hundreds of vendors, middleware providers, and abstraction layers. This means a safer, more transparent, and less costly banking supply chain. Come build with us!




THE OPPORTUNITY

Column's Growth team is focused on managing our highly strategic customer relationships. As a member of the Growth team, you’ll be responsible for building and overseeing all aspects of our customer management while acting as a trusted partner, advisor and advocate for a wide variety of customers. You will also be in charge of designing and implementing the strategy, processes, and content needed to help us scale this key function.

Column's Growth team is not your typical Customer Success or Account Management team in that Column is a critical, often existential, partner for our customers, our customer relationships are deeply collaborative and complex, and each customer contributes meaningfully to our revenue. We're therefore looking for team members who are analytical, demonstrate great critical thinking, and have experience managing very strategic relationships.

This role is highly cross-functional and collaborative, partnering closely with Column’s Executive, Solutions Engineering, Sales, Compliance, and Product teams to ensure customers are onboarded smoothly, that we can anticipate and address both issues and opportunities for growth, and that these relationships are healthy and mutually beneficial. You’ll also represent the voice of the customer to help inform Column's strategy, including the product roadmap, go-to-market motions and more.

Success in this role also means having a desire to become an expert on the Financial Technology space and on how companies partner with Column (our customers include many key industry players such as Brex and Carta). This role reports to the Head of Growth and is an in-person role, where you’ll be expected to work out of our San Francisco Presidio-based office 3+ days a week.




WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Help establish our customer-facing strategy, processes, playbooks, systems and best practices. Identify patterns and build repeatability at every step

 - Manage all aspects of a customer’s relationship with Column, from onboarding and implementation to ongoing management and expansion of the account

 - Build and nurture relationships across various functions within a customer's organization, identifying and creating advocates wherever you go, and helping find new use cases for partnering with Column

 - Proactively identify challenges and opportunities for growth, and collaborate with internal and external stakeholders to find solutions in order to move the relationship forward

 - Share key findings and recommendations on market feedback and trends to inform Column’s growth strategy and product roadmap

 - Perform day-to-day activities consistent with safe and sound business practices and regulatory requirements


WHAT YOU’LL NEED TO BE SUCCESSFUL

 - 5+ years of relevant work experience -- strong preference for a mix of Consulting, Private Equity, Investment Banking, VC or an equivalent role plus operating ex

source_jobs.title: Customer Risk Strategy
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT COLUMN

For companies building financial technology and transforming the financial services space, the biggest bottleneck to their growth and innovation is often the underlying banks and infrastructure stack they rely on. We have spent our careers founding and scaling companies like Plaid, Square, Meta, Blend, and Affirm, and have seen this problem firsthand — builders and developers needing to partner with traditional banks, and creating API and abstraction layers over the patchwork that is the bank, its core, and many other vendors. All of this results in a complex (and often expensive) banking supply chain involving a user, fintech, BaaS middleware provider, bank, core and the Federal Reserve.


At Column, we set out to simplify and fix this. We are a bank and a software company built from the ground up, offering builders and developers technology-forward banking solutions that cut out the hundreds of vendors, middleware providers, and abstraction layers. This means a safer, more transparent, and less costly banking supply chain. Come build with us!




THE OPPORTUNITY

We’re looking for a high performing business athlete to join our Customer Risk Strategy team! You don’t need to be an expert in banking or risk on day one - we are seeking a driven candidate ready to disrupt financial services and willing to utilize their highly strategic, operational, and product-oriented mindset to help Column deepen its relationships with its fintech partners. In this role you will manage our forward-thinking, strategic, and highly efficient risk programs (a critical differentiator for Column). This is a unique entry point into a high-growth fintech company, where you’ll command a level of influence that few other roles can offer.

This role is for someone who thrives on taking ownership, blending strategic problem-solving with hands-on execution. Every project you lead will directly influence our ability to grow Column's business relationships so they remain strong and resilient.

This role will report directly to our Head of Regulatory Affairs and will partner closely with leaders across engineering, business, legal, compliance, operations, and go-to-market teams. This role is based in-person at our Presidio office in San Francisco (3-4 days/week).



WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Manage oversight of Column’s Fintech Partners: Ensure effective ongoing oversight processes by reviewing key program metrics to identify trends, outliers or key risk indicators that require further investigation.

 - Evaluate partnership expansions. Work with our business team to manage fintech partnership expansions, collaborating with internal SMEs to evaluate and document key risk and compliance considerations.

 - Architect new processes: Identify inefficiencies and build new, high-velocity processes as our partnership footprint expands.

 - Drive intelligence and reporting: Deliver high-impact metrics and briefings to senior leadership and the Board of Directors, providing the data needed to make critical business decisions.


WHAT YOU’LL NEED TO BE SUCCESSFUL

 - 2+ years of experience in high-growth environments, including management consulting, financial regulatory agency, financial services and/or fintech

 - Ability to jump into new situations, master them quickly, and solve problems with minimal friction

 - Disciplined approach to to managing complex workstreams and the ability to navigate high-pressure priorities across multiple teams and projects

 - Scrappy, roll-up-your-sleeves attitude - we thrive because we work harder, are more creative, and have zero ego when it comes to getting our hands dirty to build a business

 - Attention to detail and a commitment to accuracy

 - Familiarity with the banking and fintech industries is preferred, but not required - an interest in finance, risk, and technology, however, is a must

 - Excitement about working in a fast-paced, deeply cross-functional space




WHAT YOU’LL GET FROM US:

 - 🏥 Comprehensive healt

source_jobs.title: Strategic Partnerships Lead
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT COLUMN

For companies building financial technology and transforming the financial services space, the biggest bottleneck to their growth and innovation is often the underlying banks and infrastructure stack they rely on. We have spent our careers founding and scaling companies like Plaid, Square, Meta, Blend, and Affirm, and have seen this problem firsthand — builders and developers needing to partner with traditional banks, and creating API and abstraction layers over the patchwork that is the bank, its core, and many other vendors. All of this results in a complex (and often expensive) banking supply chain involving a user, fintech, BaaS middleware provider, bank, core and the Federal Reserve.


At Column, we set out to simplify and fix this. We are a bank and a software company built from the ground up, offering builders and developers technology-forward banking solutions that cut out the hundreds of vendors, middleware providers, and abstraction layers. This means a safer, more transparent, and less costly banking supply chain. Come build with us!




THE OPPORTUNITY

We're hiring a strategic deals person to own high-value relationship development and deal execution across Column's most important partnerships. You'll drive the full commercial cycle — from identifying and qualifying strategic accounts to structuring and closing agreements with companies building financial products on Column's API infrastructure. Working closely with leadership and the broader Business team, you'll develop a deep understanding of each prospect's product roadmap and articulate Column's value proposition and differentiation at every stage of the deal. Success looks like a portfolio of trusted, long-term partnerships and a consistent track record of closing complex, high-impact deals.

We win on speed and expertise. If you move fast, love becoming an expert, and are able to build relationships and influence quickly, this opportunity might be for you.

This role reports to the Head of Sales & Partnerships and is based in San Francisco or remote, with ~50% travel expected, always dependent on deal needs.




WHAT YOU’LL DO:


DEAL EXECUTION

 - Run the full sales cycle: discovery, scoping, negotiation, and close across complex, multi-stakeholder organizations.

 - Navigate procurement, compliance, legal, and technical stakeholders simultaneously; proactively identify and overcome blockers before they stall deals.

 - Partner with Solutions Engineering, Legal, Risk, and Compliance to structure solutions that address novel customer use cases.


MARKET & PRODUCT EXPERTISE

 - Develop deep fluency in Column's API suite and how it applies across financial services use cases; position yourself as a credible thought partner for prospects' Product and Compliance teams.

 - Maintain a strong pulse on market dynamics, competitive positioning, and regulatory developments affecting Column's target customers.


PIPELINE DEVELOPMENT

 - Build and maintain account plans with clear milestones, stakeholder maps, and commercial targets.

 - Source and qualify new opportunities through outbound prospecting, industry relationships, and market intelligence — you're building pipeline, not waiting for it.


TEAM & PLAYBOOK BUILDING

 - Contribute to the development of Column's sales playbook — deal structures, qualification frameworks, and processes that the broader team can learn from and build on.

 - Operate in accordance with safe and sound business practices and applicable regulatory requirements.


WHAT YOU’LL NEED TO BE SUCCESSFUL:

 - 8+ years in strategic partnerships, business development, strategic accounts sales, or a commercial-adjacent function, including investors or capital markets professionals who have spent meaningful time sourcing, evaluating, and closing complex deals with sophisticated counterparties.

 - Proven ability to run structured, autonomous deal cycles from pipeline generation through close

 - Demonstrated ability to navigate larg

source_jobs.title: Customer Engineer
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT COLUMN

For companies building financial technology and transforming the financial services space, the biggest bottleneck to their growth and innovation is often the underlying banks and infrastructure stack they rely on. We have spent our careers founding and scaling companies like Plaid, Square, Meta, Blend, and Affirm, and have seen this problem firsthand — builders and developers needing to partner with traditional banks, and creating API and abstraction layers over the patchwork that is the bank, its core, and many other vendors. All of this results in a complex (and often expensive) banking supply chain involving a user, fintech, BaaS middleware provider, bank, core and the Federal Reserve.


At Column, we set out to simplify and fix this. We are a bank and a software company built from the ground up, offering builders and developers technology-forward banking solutions that cut out the hundreds of vendors, middleware providers, and abstraction layers. This means a safer, more transparent, and less costly banking supply chain. Come build with us!




THE OPPORTUNITY:

As a Customer Engineer at Column, you'll partner with tech-forward companies to build financial products and solutions using Column's APIs. This is a highly cross-functional role: you'll work closely with engineering, business, operations, legal, and compliance teams to support customer implementations and expand customer activity on Column—directly contributing to the growth of our business.

This role is more technically demanding than the average Customer Engineering position. You'll need to develop a deep understanding of our platform and APIs to serve as a trusted technical partner to customers. Beyond customer implementation, you'll gather and synthesize customer feedback, identify product gaps, and help shape new product requirements, working directly with our engineering team to drive product development.

You'll also be responsible for building internal tools and automation to improve our team's operational efficiency. You will have significant opportunities to expand your scope and take on additional responsibility as you grow.

This role is based in our Presidio office in San Francisco and follows a four-day, in-person schedule. You will report to Column's Head of Solution Engineering.




WHAT YOU'LL DO:

 - Support the technical relationship with our customers across pre-sales, implementation, onboarding, and support.

 - Assist with the implementation and delivery of strategic cross-functional projects with guidance from senior team members.

 - Leverage AI to build internal tooling and processes to address operational bottlenecks

 - Collect and organize product feedback, help identify gaps, and support the writing of product requirement documents.

 - Learn to manage roadmap expectations with our engineering team and customers.

 - Perform day-to-day activities consistent with safe and sound business practices and regulatory requirements.

 - Help identify, assess, and manage risk by adhering to policies and procedures established by the Bank, and report issues to management.
   
   


WHAT YOU'LL NEED TO BE SUCCESSFUL:

 - 3+ years of experience in a technical or technical-adjacent role

 - Strong customer-facing presence

 - Clear written and verbal communication skills

 - Problem-solving aptitude and curiosity

 - Humble attitude and eagerness to roll up your sleeves

 - Basic technical skills with potential to grow in coding (JavaScript or Python) and ability to learn SQL

 - Interest in financial services industry (prior knowledge is helpful but not required)
   
   


WHAT YOU'LL GET FROM US:

 - 🏥 Comprehensive health, dental, and vision plans, including options that are 100% covered by Column for you and 100% covered for your dependents!

 - 🐣 Comprehensive family planning and fertility benefits via partnership with Carrot, including reimbursement of up to $20,000 in qualified expenses

 - 🏡 Up to $2,000 (post-tax) monthly ren

source_jobs.title: Events and Brand Experience Lead
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT COLUMN

For companies building financial technology and transforming the financial services space, the biggest bottleneck to their growth and innovation is often the underlying banks and infrastructure stack they rely on. We have spent our careers founding and scaling companies like Plaid, Square, Meta, Blend, and Affirm, and have seen this problem firsthand — builders and developers needing to partner with traditional banks, and creating API and abstraction layers over the patchwork that is the bank, its core, and many other vendors. All of this results in a complex (and often expensive) banking supply chain involving a user, fintech, BaaS middleware provider, bank, core and the Federal Reserve.


At Column, we set out to simplify and fix this. We are a bank and a software company built from the ground up, offering builders and developers technology-forward banking solutions that cut out the hundreds of vendors, middleware providers, and abstraction layers. This means a safer, more transparent, and less costly banking supply chain. Come build with us!




THE OPPORTUNITY

As our integral Events and Brand Experiences Lead, you'll own every important moment Column creates: the dinners that turn prospects into partners, the offsites that shape our culture, the conference presence that signals who we are, and the customer gatherings that make people feel proud to be building with us. You'll work directly with our CEO, our Go-to-Market team, and our Workplace Experience team to make all of it exceptional.


This is a high-ownership role with real budget, direct executive access, and no bureaucracy between you and the work. If you're someone with operational excellence who has managed large budgets, sweated every detail, and knows that a well-executed event is a foundational relationship-building tool, this is a rare opportunity to build a function from the ground up at a company that values excellent experiences.



This role is based in our San Francisco HQ, reports into our Head of People, and is expected to be in-person 2-3 days/week. Regular travel to conferences and events (domestic and international) should be expected.




WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Own strategy and execution for Column's highest-stakes internal and external moments — customer-facing events, company offsites, board meetings, brand activations, and conference presence.

 - Partner with our Workplace Experience Manager to build employee engagement programming that reflects and reinforces Column's culture.

 - Run the full production stack: vendor sourcing, venue selection, creative direction, communications, budget management, and day-of execution.

 - Work with GTM to develop event strategies that generate pipeline and deepen relationships with the people who matter most to our business.

 - Be on the ground at high-priority events — present, adaptable, and always three steps ahead.

 - Contribute to broader marketing campaigns and brand initiatives as Column continues to grow.




WHAT YOU’LL NEED TO BE SUCCESSFUL

 - 5+ years of experience in event production, experiential marketing, or brand activation roles—ideally at a high-growth startup or creative agency.

 - Demonstrated success executing high-profile, detail-rich events under tight timelines.

 - Outstanding project management skills and ability to multitask in fast-paced environments.

 - A strong aesthetic sensibility and eye for brand cohesion.

 - Scrappy, resourceful, and humble—you’re just as comfortable building a strategy deck as you are taping down cables.

 - Excellent written and verbal communication skills; comfort interfacing with executives and VIPs.

 - Willingness to travel and adapt on the fly.




WHAT YOU'LL GET FROM US

 - 🏥 Comprehensive health, dental, and vision plans, including options that are 100% covered by Column for you and 100% covered for your dependents!

 - 👶 Comprehensive family planning and fertility benefits via partnership with Carrot, including reimbursement of u

source_jobs.title: Digital Assets Compliance
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT COLUMN

For companies building financial technology and transforming the financial services space, the biggest bottleneck to their growth and innovation is often the underlying banks and infrastructure stack they rely on. We have spent our careers founding and scaling companies like Plaid, Square, Meta, Blend, and Affirm, and have seen this problem firsthand — builders and developers needing to partner with traditional banks, and creating API and abstraction layers over the patchwork that is the bank, its core, and many other vendors. All of this results in a complex (and often expensive) banking supply chain involving a user, fintech, BaaS middleware provider, bank, core and the Federal Reserve.


At Column, we set out to simplify and fix this. We are a bank and a software company built from the ground up, offering builders and developers technology-forward banking solutions that cut out the hundreds of vendors, middleware providers, and abstraction layers. This means a safer, more transparent, and less costly banking supply chain. Come build with us!




THE OPPORTUNITY

Column is hiring for a Digital Assets Compliance role to define, build, and operate Column's compliance framework for its crypto products. The GENIUS Act and other federal guidelines have started to clarify how banks can provide digital asset services. Against this backdrop, Column is looking for someone to support the ongoing development of practical systems that protect the bank while enabling innovation. This isn't a box-checking compliance role. We need a builder who understands that compliance done right is a competitive advantage — it unlocks partnerships, accelerates product velocity, and builds trust with regulators. You'll work directly with our product, engineering, and risk teams to manage systems that scale, not just procedures that look good on paper. If you have experience building programs and tooling, and monitoring and analyzing cryptocurrency transactions using best-in-class blockchain investigations tools, we want to talk to you.

What this role is: This role will serve as a subject matter expert with deep specialization in digital assets compliance. You'll be embedded within our financial crimes team, developing the Bank's standards for monitoring and controlling the AML and sanctions risks inherent in the bank's stablecoin infrastructure, payment rails, and crypto-enabled banking products.

What this role is NOT: This is not a securities or broker-dealer compliance role. Many crypto compliance professionals to date have focused on securities regulations for trading platforms and custodial services, but we're not looking for expertise in custody, capital markets, or investment products. We need someone who understands the AML and sanctions tooling and controls for payments, and how digital assets interact with traditional payment systems — not someone who's spent their career navigating SEC rules for crypto exchanges.

This position will report directly to the Bank's Chief Compliance Officer, and the individual in the role must be willing to work in person in our Presidio-based office in San Francisco (3-4 days/week).




WHAT YOU’LL DO





OPERATE COMPLIANCE INFRASTRUCTURE

 - Operate the AML/BSA monitoring capabilities for stablecoin and crypto payment products in accordance with the GENIUS Act and Bank Secrecy Act requirements and the Bank's existing AML program

 - Refine and maintain a comprehensive sanctions compliance program for digital assets, including blockchain analytics, wallet screening, and sanctions risk management specific to crypto payment flows

 - Expand our existing suspicious activity monitoring and sanctions screening tools and processes to blend digital asset-specific workflows

 - Develop, test, and tune new blockchain monitoring rules

 - Own monthly metric reporting on stablecoin-specific activity

 - Build out stablecoin-specific operational policies and procedures

 - Stay on top of emerging risk trends

source_jobs.title: Commercial Counsel
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT COLUMN

For companies building financial technology and transforming the financial services space, the biggest bottleneck to their growth and innovation is often the underlying banks and infrastructure stack they rely on. We have spent our careers founding and scaling companies like Plaid, Square, Meta, Blend, and Affirm, and have seen this problem firsthand — builders and developers needing to partner with traditional banks, and creating API and abstraction layers over the patchwork that is the bank, its core, and many other vendors. All of this results in a complex (and often expensive) banking supply chain involving a user, fintech, BaaS middleware provider, bank, core and the Federal Reserve.


At Column, we set out to simplify and fix this. We are a bank and a software company built from the ground up, offering builders and developers technology-forward banking solutions that cut out the hundreds of vendors, middleware providers, and abstraction layers. This means a safer, more transparent, and less costly banking supply chain. Come build with us!




THE OPPORTUNITY

Column is growing its in-house commercial legal team to own the legal infrastructure behind our most important commercial and strategic relationships. This isn't your typical in-house commercial counsel role reviewing SaaS contracts - you'll architect novel partnership structures that enable modern banking experiences, navigating complex regulatory requirements along the way. You'll work closely with Column's Sales & Partnerships, Risk, and Compliance teams to define how we structure our deals, serve our customers, and scale our platform safely. If you're excited about combining legal acumen with a builder's mindset - and want you work to have a direct impact on how financial services are delivered at scale - we think you'll be excited about this role!

This is a hybrid in-person role based in our Presidio office — one of the most beautiful locations in SF — 3–4+ days per week. We offer up to $2,000/month in post-tax rent subsidy for employees who live within 2 miles of our office and welcome candidates who would relocate.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Own complex agreements end-to-end: Structure, draft, and negotiate complex customer, and vendor, and third-party agreements covering a broad range of banking products and services, including agreements related to payments and money movement service and banking partnerships inclusive of deposit account, card, and lending programs.

 - Build and manage legal playbooks and templates that scale: Develop and maintain templates and inventories of contracts, terms of services, notices, and disclosures for the Bank’s products, services, and distribution channels that enable speed without sacrificing quality.

 - Negotiate: Lead negotiations with high-growth fintechs and enterprise customers, balancing commercial priorities with regulatory requirements.

 - Develop strong cross-functional partnerships: Work closely with the Sales and Business teams to understand the Bank’s goals, strategy, and risk tolerance for each deal in order to develop an efficient and risk-based contracting strategy.

 - Expand into additional areas of law: As the fourth member of our growing Legal team, you'll support each other with novel research, product and regulatory advice, exam management, subpoena responses, litigation deliverables, and corporate governance materials.

 - Help us level up how Legal operates: Serve as a thought partner to the business and legal to support Column's ongoing growth through improvements to bank agreements, policies, and legal processes.




WHAT YOU'LL NEED TO BE SUCCESSFUL

 - 5+ years as a practicing lawyer; priority given to candidates with law firm experience in a technology transactions or fintech practice and/or in-house experience at a fintech or financial institution

 - Ability to independently draft and negotiate commercial agreements, with a strong understanding of both contract law and commercial

source_jobs.title: Emerging Markets Lead – Central Asia (Russian speaking)
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT COLUMN

For companies building financial technology and transforming the financial services space, the biggest bottleneck to their growth and innovation is often the underlying banks and infrastructure stack they rely on. We have spent our careers founding and scaling companies like Plaid, Square, Meta, Blend, and Affirm, and have seen this problem firsthand — builders and developers needing to partner with traditional banks, and creating API and abstraction layers over the patchwork that is the bank, its core, and many other vendors. All of this results in a complex (and often expensive) banking supply chain involving a user, fintech, BaaS middleware provider, bank, core and the Federal Reserve.


At Column, we set out to simplify and fix this. We are a bank and a software company built from the ground up, offering builders and developers technology-forward banking solutions that cut out the hundreds of vendors, middleware providers, and abstraction layers. This means a safer, more transparent, and less costly banking supply chain. Come build with us!




THE OPPORTUNITY

As Column’s Emerging Markets Lead, you will spearhead one of our most strategic international expansion initiatives. In this role, you will be responsible for driving 0-to-1 growth for a new banking product by building and nurturing relationships with prospective partners and customers in select markets, with a specific focus on Eastern Europe and Central Asia. This role combines global expansion strategy, business development with hands-on relationship management, requiring a general business athlete who thrives on networking and executing complex partnerships. This is a unique opportunity to be on the founding team for a new business line, with significant autonomy and direct impact on Column’s global expansion strategy.

This position will be based in Dubai, with regular travel throughout the region and quarterly travel to Column’s headquarters in San Francisco.




WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Lead regional market development: Own the end-to-end process of identifying, evaluating, and executing on high-value partnerships with banks across a number of international markets.

 - Build and maintain key relationships: Serve as the primary relationship owner for prospective and existing customers, conducting regular in-person meetings, attending local industry events, and managing ongoing partnership development.

 - Own partnership execution: Lead complex deal processes from initial outreach through contract signature, working collaboratively with Column’s Legal, Compliance, and Product teams.

 - Develop market intelligence: Gather and synthesize insights on local market conditions, regulatory developments, competitive landscape, and customer needs to inform Column’s regional strategy.

 - Market expertise and network: Become an expert on the correspondent banking space in our key emerging markets and build an extensive network within their banking and fintech ecosystems.

 - Drive operational excellence: Build scalable processes for onboarding, relationship management, and ongoing support that can be replicated as we expand.




WHAT YOU’LL NEED TO BE SUCCESSFUL

 - 6+ years of experience in business operations, growth or partnerships in a high-caliber environment; prior experience at a top-tier technology company or management consulting firm preferred

 - 4+ years of business experience specifically in Central Asian and Eastern European markets

 - Native fluency in Russian and experience managing business relationships with Russian-speaking clients and stakeholders

 - Track record of successfully managing complex partnerships and high-value deals with executive stakeholders

 - Natural networker who thrives on building trust and long-term relationships with senior executives, market experts and decision-makers

 - Strong strategic thinking - ability to assess market opportunities, evaluate potential partners, and provide strategic recommendations to senior

source_jobs.title: Risk Operations
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT COLUMN

For companies building financial technology and transforming the financial services space, the biggest bottleneck to their growth and innovation is often the underlying banks and infrastructure stack they rely on. We have spent our careers founding and scaling companies like Plaid, Square, Meta, Blend, and Affirm, and have seen this problem firsthand — builders and developers needing to partner with traditional banks, and creating API and abstraction layers over the patchwork that is the bank, its core, and many other vendors. All of this results in a complex (and often expensive) banking supply chain involving a user, fintech, BaaS middleware provider, bank, core and the Federal Reserve.


At Column, we set out to simplify and fix this. We are a bank and a software company built from the ground up, offering builders and developers technology-forward banking solutions that cut out the hundreds of vendors, middleware providers, and abstraction layers. This means a safer, more transparent, and less costly banking supply chain. Come build with us!




THE OPPORTUNITY

Many banks treat Risk as a cost center - Column treats it as a competitive advantage. Our ability to take on new customers, expand into new product areas, and successfully operate under regulatory scrutiny depends entirely on the quality of our Risk programs. As we grow, the volume and complexity of that work will outpace what the team can absorb - that’s where this role comes in.

This is a strategic operations role that lives inside our Risk function. It’s less about being a Risk expert and more about being an exceptional operator who is energized by learning new domains quickly, spotting inefficiencies, and building the tools to fix them. You’ll work closely with a variety of teams across Column (e.g., Engineering, People, Compliance, Legal, etc.) to figure out which processes and programs are slowing us down. By the time you’re done with a project, tasks that took days should take minutes, and you’ll have a deep understanding of what it takes to operate one of the most strategically important functions to our business.

This role reports to the Head of Technology Risk and is in-person based out of our San Francisco Presidio office 3-4 days a week.




WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Plug into whichever Risk program needs the most support at a given moment - third-party risk management, information security, model risk, privacy, financial, operational, etc. - and figure out what’s broken, slow, or missing.

 - Learn each program from the ground up - understand what it’s actually protecting against, how it works in practice, and where the gaps are. Then re-design it so it’s faster and more consistent.

 - Build the tooling that makes the next version of a process possible - scripts, internal dashboards, AI-assisted review workflows, system integrations.

 - Support third-party diligence and monitoring, helping assess vendor/supplier risk and turning your findings into concrete remediation steps.

 - Help design business continuity and disaster recovery (BC/DR) exercises that test how Column and its partners actually respond when things go wrong.

 - Take our privacy program to the next level, and make sure privacy considerations are part of the way we diligence prospective customers from the start.
   
   


WHAT YOU’LL NEED TO BE SUCCESSFUL

 - 2+ years of doing something hard. This could be in consulting, technical operations, software engineering, or any role where you had to make sense of a complex system, improve it, and get others to adopt the change. Prior risk experience is not required - we’ll teach you the domain.

 - You get up to speed fast. You’re the person who can be handed an unfamiliar problem, spend a week understanding it, and come back with a point of view.

 - You have an optimization instinct. When you see a manual process, your first question is "could this be automated?" and you have the skills to actually build the tooling necessary to ma

source_jobs.title: Product Manager, Platform Tools
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT COLUMN

For companies building financial technology and transforming the financial services space, the biggest bottleneck to their growth and innovation is often the underlying banks and infrastructure stack they rely on. We have spent our careers founding and scaling companies like Plaid, Square, Meta, Blend, and Affirm, and have seen this problem firsthand — builders and developers needing to partner with traditional banks, and creating API and abstraction layers over the patchwork that is the bank, its core, and many other vendors. All of this results in a complex (and often expensive) banking supply chain involving a user, fintech, BaaS middleware provider, bank, core and the Federal Reserve.


At Column, we set out to simplify and fix this. We are a bank and a software company built from the ground up, offering builders and developers technology-forward banking solutions that cut out the hundreds of vendors, middleware providers, and abstraction layers. This means a safer, more transparent, and less costly banking supply chain. Come build with us!




THE OPPORTUNITY

Column processes over $2 trillion in annual transaction volume for some of the most sophisticated tech companies and financial institutions in the world. Keeping that volume safe is one of the most consequential jobs in fintech. We're looking for a high-agency product leader to own our risk analytics platform: the internal infrastructure that lets Column grow without creating openings for fraudsters, criminal networks, or state adversaries probing the US financial system.

You'll turn a traditionally manual function into a scalable internal product, working directly with our senior leaders to shape how Column monitors, investigates, and governs risk at scale. As we expand into new products and markets, this platform is what makes that growth possible. This role is based in-person at our Presidio office in San Francisco (3-4 days/week).

 


WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Own the product roadmap for financial crimes monitoring, analytics, workflow, and governance tooling — turning a traditionally manual and compliance-heavy function into a scalable internal product

 - Productize the transaction monitoring scenario library across products, customer types, geographies, and flow-of-funds models

 - Translate emerging risks, regulatory guidance, internal risk assessments, and observed trends into clear monitoring and tooling requirements; design the full process for rule development, testing, deployment, tuning, and model management

 - Partner with risk teams, engineering, GTM, and business operations to design practical controls before new products or customer activity launch

 - Develop dashboards and analytics that help the team understand risk coverage, alert quality, control performance, investigation outcomes, and operational bottlenecks

 - Lead annual and ongoing risk coverage assessments across customers, products, geographies, and typologies

 - Work closely with our CEO, CCO, and General Counsel to shape adjacent risk workflows — and build toward a customer-facing program management solution over time

 


WHAT YOU’LL NEED TO BE SUCCESSFUL

 - 4+ years in a high agency, fast moving, high intellect role. Examples could be product management or adjacent role at a high-growth startup or tech company; top-tier management consulting firm; or investment firm

 - Strong product judgment: the ability to turn ambiguous problems into clear requirements and shipped solutions

 - Ability to get technical very quickly. While we don’t expect you to be a CS major, you should feel comfortable in the terminal, using Claude code or similar tools, and understanding how to architect solutions out yourself.

 - Strong analytical instincts — comfortable working with data, metrics, and operational details at a granular level

 - Aptitude for building credibility with subject matter experts across engineering, risk & compliance, and business operations

 - Strong written communic

source_jobs.title: Strategic Partnerships Lead, Credit
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT COLUMN

For companies building financial technology and transforming the financial services space, the biggest bottleneck to their growth and innovation is often the underlying banks and infrastructure stack they rely on. We have spent our careers founding and scaling companies like Plaid, Square, Meta, Blend, and Affirm, and have seen this problem firsthand — builders and developers needing to partner with traditional banks, and creating API and abstraction layers over the patchwork that is the bank, its core, and many other vendors. All of this results in a complex (and often expensive) banking supply chain involving a user, fintech, BaaS middleware provider, bank, core and the Federal Reserve.


At Column, we set out to simplify and fix this. We are a bank and a software company built from the ground up, offering builders and developers technology-forward banking solutions that cut out the hundreds of vendors, middleware providers, and abstraction layers. This means a safer, more transparent, and less costly banking supply chain. Come build with us!




THE OPPORTUNITY

We're hiring a strategic deals person to own high-value relationship development and deal execution across Column's credit partnerships. You'll drive the full commercial cycle — from identifying and qualifying strategic accounts to structuring and closing agreements with companies building financial products on Column's API infrastructure. Working closely with leadership and the broader Business team, you'll develop a deep understanding of each prospect's product roadmap and articulate Column's value proposition and differentiation at every stage of the deal. Success looks like a portfolio of trusted, long-term partnerships and a consistent track record of closing complex, high-impact deals. For this credit-focused role, you must have a background in credit and lending, capital markets, structured finance or a similar field.

We win on speed and expertise. If you move fast, love becoming an expert, and are able to build relationships and influence quickly, this opportunity might be for you.

This role reports to the Head of Sales & Partnerships and is based in San Francisco or remote, with ~50% travel expected, always dependent on deal needs.




WHAT YOU’LL DO:


DEAL EXECUTION

 - Run the full sales cycle: discovery, scoping, negotiation, and close across complex, multi-stakeholder organizations; evaluate opportunities to take credit risk alongside our partners.

 - Navigate procurement, compliance, legal, and technical stakeholders simultaneously; proactively identify and overcome blockers before they stall deals.

 - Partner with Solutions Engineering, Legal, Risk, and Compliance to structure solutions that address novel customer use cases.


MARKET & PRODUCT EXPERTISE

 - Develop deep fluency in Column's API suite and how it applies across financial services use cases; position yourself as a credible thought partner for prospects' Product and Compliance teams.

 - Maintain a strong pulse on market dynamics, competitive positioning, and regulatory developments affecting Column's target customers.


PIPELINE DEVELOPMENT

 - Build and maintain account plans with clear milestones, stakeholder maps, and commercial targets.

 - Source and qualify new opportunities through outbound prospecting, industry relationships, and market intelligence — you're building pipeline, not waiting for it.


TEAM & PLAYBOOK BUILDING

 - Contribute to the development of Column's sales playbook — deal structures, qualification frameworks, and processes that the broader team can learn from and build on.

 - Operate in accordance with safe and sound business practices and applicable regulatory requirements.


WHAT YOU’LL NEED TO BE SUCCESSFUL:

 - 8+ years in strategic partnerships, business development, strategic accounts sales, or a commercial-adjacent function, including investors or capital markets professionals who have spent meaningful time sourcing, evaluating, and clo

source_jobs.title: Emerging Markets Lead – Latin America (Spanish speaking)
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT COLUMN

For companies building financial technology and transforming the financial services space, the biggest bottleneck to their growth and innovation is often the underlying banks and infrastructure stack they rely on. We have spent our careers founding and scaling companies like Plaid, Square, Meta, Blend, and Affirm, and have seen this problem firsthand — builders and developers needing to partner with traditional banks, and creating API and abstraction layers over the patchwork that is the bank, its core, and many other vendors. All of this results in a complex (and often expensive) banking supply chain involving a user, fintech, BaaS middleware provider, bank, core and the Federal Reserve.


At Column, we set out to simplify and fix this. We are a bank and a software company built from the ground up, offering builders and developers technology-forward banking solutions that cut out the hundreds of vendors, middleware providers, and abstraction layers. This means a safer, more transparent, and less costly banking supply chain. Come build with us!




THE OPPORTUNITY

As Column’s Emerging Markets Lead, you will spearhead one of our most strategic international expansion initiatives. In this role, you will be responsible for driving 0-to-1 growth for a new banking product by building and nurturing relationships with prospective partners and customers in select markets, with a specific focus on Latin America. This role combines global expansion strategy, business development with hands-on relationship management, requiring a general business athlete who thrives on networking and executing complex partnerships. This is a unique opportunity to be on the founding team for a new business line, with significant autonomy and direct impact on Column’s global expansion strategy.

This position will require regular travel throughout the region and has the flexibility to be based in Latin America with quarterly travel to Column’s headquarters in San Francisco.




WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Lead regional market development: Own the end-to-end process of identifying, evaluating, and executing on high-value partnerships with banks across a number of international markets.

 - Build and maintain key relationships: Serve as the primary relationship owner for prospective and existing customers, conducting regular in-person meetings, attending local industry events, and managing ongoing partnership development.

 - Own partnership execution: Lead complex deal processes from initial outreach through contract signature, working collaboratively with Column’s Legal, Compliance, and Product teams.

 - Develop market intelligence: Gather and synthesize insights on local market conditions, regulatory developments, competitive landscape, and customer needs to inform Column’s regional strategy.

 - Market expertise and network: Become an expert on the correspondent banking space in our key emerging markets and build an extensive network within their banking and fintech ecosystems.

 - Drive operational excellence: Build scalable processes for onboarding, relationship management, and ongoing support that can be replicated as we expand.




WHAT YOU’LL NEED TO BE SUCCESSFUL

 - 6+ years of experience in business operations, growth or partnerships in a high-caliber environment; prior experience at a top-tier technology company or management consulting firm preferred

 - 4+ years of experience conducting business in Latin America

 - Native fluency in Spanish and experience managing business relationships with Spanish-speaking clients and stakeholders

 - Track record of successfully managing complex partnerships and high-value deals with executive stakeholders

 - Natural networker who thrives on building trust and long-term relationships with senior executives, market experts and decision-makers

 - Strong strategic thinking - ability to assess market opportunities, evaluate potential partners, and provide strategic recommendations to senior leadership

source_jobs.title: Senior Counsel, Strategic Partnerships
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT COLUMN

For companies building financial technology and transforming the financial services space, the biggest bottleneck to their growth and innovation is often the underlying banks and infrastructure stack they rely on. We have spent our careers founding and scaling companies like Plaid, Square, Meta, Blend, and Affirm, and have seen this problem firsthand — builders and developers needing to partner with traditional banks, and creating API and abstraction layers over the patchwork that is the bank, its core, and many other vendors. All of this results in a complex (and often expensive) banking supply chain involving a user, fintech, BaaS middleware provider, bank, core and the Federal Reserve.


At Column, we set out to simplify and fix this. We are a bank and a software company built from the ground up, offering builders and developers technology-forward banking solutions that cut out the hundreds of vendors, middleware providers, and abstraction layers. This means a safer, more transparent, and less costly banking supply chain. Come build with us!




THE OPPORTUNITY

At Column, we’re looking for a senior lawyer excited to own strategic partnerships end to end, from the first "we want to build with Column" conversation through signing, product development, launch, and ongoing expansion. Instead of handing the work off to a relationship manager or a separate product team, you're the through-line. You will architect novel partnership structures that enable fintechs to deliver modern banking experiences, navigating complex regulatory requirements along the way. You'll work closely with Go-to-Market, Engineering, Finance, Risk and Compliance and have a direct impact on how bespoke financial products are built and delivered at scale. Column’s product suite spans accounts, payments, cards, lending, and loan participations, delivered via both domestic and international fintechs to consumers and businesses.

This role offers direct access to leadership, will involve daily ongoing work with our external partners, and requires creativity and speed. The Column Legal team does not have Product, Regulatory, and Commercial silos – we believe the most effective lawyers are those who can own partnership launches and expansion end-to-end. You will have access to the best AI tools and Legal Ops support.

This role reports directly to the Bank’s General Counsel.




WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Structure Bank-Fintech Partnerships: Lead the legal work to structure and launch partnerships across both payments and credit. Advise on line of credit products, bank accounts, consumer and commercial loan origination, multi-currency wallets, domestic and international card issuance, EWA and BNPL products, complex global money movement products, and more. Draft and review key customer-facing terms and marketing materials, structure onboarding flows and applications, determine BSA/AML requirements, conduct regulatory analyses, and own every legal detail of rolling out a new Column-backed product. Work closely with engineering to design a flow-of-funds model and reflect that product structure in legal documentation.

 - Own complex agreements end-to-end: Structure, draft, and negotiate complex customer and third-party partnership agreements covering a broad range of banking products and services.

 - Drive Product Strategy & Innovation: Be the strategic legal voice enabling product innovation. Partner closely with business teams and fintech partners from ideation through launch, anticipating challenges and architecting creative solutions that balance business needs with regulatory requirements. Advise on product structuring, and the legal implications of new lending and payments features and partnership models.

 - Own Regulatory Architecture: Build and evolve regulatory frameworks for existing and new products. Leverage your deep expertise in NACHA, money transmission, BSA/AML, TILA, Reg Z, Reg E, ECOA, UDAAP, FCRA, state usury and licensing laws, the evo

source_jobs.title: Growth, Credit & Lending Partnerships
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT COLUMN

For companies building financial technology and transforming the financial services space, the biggest bottleneck to their growth and innovation is often the underlying banks and infrastructure stack they rely on. We have spent our careers founding and scaling companies like Plaid, Square, Meta, Blend, and Affirm, and have seen this problem firsthand — builders and developers needing to partner with traditional banks, and creating API and abstraction layers over the patchwork that is the bank, its core, and many other vendors. All of this results in a complex (and often expensive) banking supply chain involving a user, fintech, BaaS middleware provider, bank, core and the Federal Reserve.


At Column, we set out to simplify and fix this. We are a bank and a software company built from the ground up, offering builders and developers technology-forward banking solutions that cut out the hundreds of vendors, middleware providers, and abstraction layers. This means a safer, more transparent, and less costly banking supply chain. Come build with us!



The opportunity

Column's Growth team is focused on managing our highly strategic customer relationships. As a member of the Growth team, you'll be responsible for building and overseeing all aspects of our customer management while acting as a trusted partner, advisor and advocate for a wide variety of customers. You will also be in charge of designing and implementing the strategy, processes, and content needed to help us scale this key function.

Column's Growth team is not your typical Customer Success or Account Management team in that Column is a critical, often existential, partner for our customers, our customer relationships are deeply collaborative and complex, and each customer contributes meaningfully to our revenue. We're therefore looking for team members who are analytical, demonstrate great critical thinking, and have experience managing very strategic relationships.

This particular role will focus on Column's customers building credit and lending products on top of Column's infrastructure. You'll bring genuine fluency in credit and capital markets to a role that's highly cross-functional and collaborative, partnering closely with Column's Executive, Sales, Compliance, and Solutions Engineering teams to ensure customers are onboarded smoothly, that we can anticipate and address both issues and opportunities for growth, and that these relationships are healthy and mutually beneficial. You'll also represent the voice of the customer to help inform Column's strategy, including the product roadmap, go-to-market motions and more.

Success in this role also means having a desire to become an expert on the Financial Technology space and on how companies partner with Column (our customers include many key industry players such as Brex and Carta). This role reports to the Head of Growth and is an in-person role, where you'll be expected to work out of our San Francisco Presidio-based office 4+ days a week.

What you'll do

 - Own and manage relationships with Column's customers from onboarding through ongoing growth and expansion, primarily focusing on customers credit and lending products

 - Apply your credit and capital markets fluency to understand customer risk profiles, reserve requirements, underwriting considerations, and lending product structures

 - Partner closely with Column's Risk, Compliance, and Solutions Engineering teams to help customers navigate the regulatory and technical complexity of building lending products on Column

 - Build the playbooks, processes, and best practices for how Column's Growth team supports credit and lending customers

 - Represent the voice of the customer internally to inform Column's product roadmap for credit and lending infrastructure

 - Perform day-to-day activities consistent with safe and sound banking practices and regulatory requirements

What you'll need to be successful

 - 2-4+ years of experience in a c

notes: Financial technology company with a mix of tech and non-tech roles
